methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $3.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 26 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $15.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Together AI, a Series G AI & Software company founded by Vipul Prakash and Ce Zhang, has raised $534M to date against a last post-money valuation of $3.3B. The valuation analysis draws on two applicable triangulation methods anchored to a peer universe of 26 same-sector, similar-valuation-tier private companies, with a single public comp (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and eight additional private reference points including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Databricks. The peer range spans $1.0B to $15.0B.

Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.25x step-up multiplier from Series G to the next funding stage, implying $4.1B. Peer-median valuation across the 26-company cohort—inclusive of Applied Intuition ($15.0B, Series F), Crusoe Energy ($10.0B, Series E), and Neuralink ($9.7B, private)—settles at $4.0B. Both methods converge tightly, with stage-progression yielding $4.1B and peer-median $4.0B.

Triangulation across these two methods supports a fair-value range of $3.4B (low) to $4.7B (high), with a median of $4.1B. The current $3.3B post-money valuation sits modestly below this range, suggesting modest upside through the next financing cycle. Key uncertainties center on execution velocity and the sustainability of unit economics relative to the broader peer cohort, where valuation dispersion ($1.0B–$15.0B) remains substantial.
